ï»¿```{r, echo=FALSE, results="hide"}
set.seed(sample.int(.Machine$integer.max, 1))

mu     <- sample(2:8, 1)
sigma2 <- sample(c(1, 4, 9), 1)
n      <- sample(c(25, 36, 49, 64, 100), 1)

correct <- round(sigma2 / n, 4)
w1      <- round(sigma2 / (n - 1), 4)
w2      <- round(sigma2 * n, 4)
w3      <- round(2 * sigma2^2 / n, 4)
w4      <- round(sigma2 / (2 * n), 4)

all_vals <- c(correct, w1, w2, w3, w4)

# Garante cinco alternativas numericamente distintas apĂ³s a parametrizaĂ§Ă£o.
integer_choices <- all(abs(all_vals - round(all_vals)) < sqrt(.Machine$double.eps))
choice_step <- if (integer_choices) 1 else max(1e-4, max(abs(all_vals), na.rm = TRUE) * 0.01)
while (anyDuplicated(all_vals)) {
  duplicate_position <- which(duplicated(all_vals))[1]
  all_vals[duplicate_position] <- max(all_vals, na.rm = TRUE) + choice_step
}
choices <- sample(all_vals)
sol_pos <- which(choices == correct)
sol_str <- paste(as.integer(seq_along(choices) == sol_pos), collapse = "")
```

Question
========
Para $X_i \overset{\text{iid}}{\sim} N(\mu, \sigma^2)$ com $\mu$ e $\sigma^2$ ambos **desconhecidos**, os EMVs sĂ£o $\hat{\mu}_{MV} = \bar{X}$ e $\hat{\sigma}^2_{MV} = \frac{1}{n}\sum(X_i - \bar{X})^2$.

A **variĂ¢ncia assintĂ³tica** de $\hat{\mu}_{MV}$ Ă© dada pela entrada $(1,1)$ de $I(\boldsymbol{\theta})^{-1}/n$, que Ă© $\sigma^2/n$.

Com $\sigma^2 = `r sigma2`$ e $n = `r n`$, qual Ă© a variĂ¢ncia assintĂ³tica de $\hat{\mu}_{MV}$?

Answerlist
----------
* `r choices[1]`
* `r choices[2]`
* `r choices[3]`
* `r choices[4]`
* `r choices[5]`

Solution
========
A matriz de informaĂ§Ă£o de Fisher para $N(\mu, \sigma^2)$ Ă© $I(\boldsymbol{\theta}) = \text{diag}(1/\sigma^2,\, 1/(2\sigma^4))$. Sua inversa Ă© $I(\boldsymbol{\theta})^{-1} = \text{diag}(\sigma^2,\, 2\sigma^4)$.

Para $n$ observaĂ§Ăµes, a variĂ¢ncia assintĂ³tica de $\hat{\mu}_{MV}$ Ă©:
$$\text{Var}_{\text{assint}}(\hat{\mu}_{MV}) = \frac{\sigma^2}{n} = \frac{`r sigma2`}{`r n`} = `r correct`.$$

Notas:
- $\sigma^2/(n-1) = `r w1`$ usaria divisĂ£o de Bessel (para $S^2$ nĂ£o-viesado, nĂ£o para variĂ¢ncia assintĂ³tica do EMV).
- $2\sigma^4/n = `r w3`$ Ă© a variĂ¢ncia assintĂ³tica de $\hat{\sigma}^2_{MV}$, nĂ£o de $\hat{\mu}_{MV}$.

Meta-information
================
exname: Cap7S1Q5_var_assint_mu_normal
extype: schoice
exsolution: `r sol_str`
exshuffle: FALSE
